front: Work: 108 spirits are released from a stone tortoise, emerging as golden rays from a black cloud at the start of this novel
Work: Marshal Hong accidentally releases the 108 imprisoned spirits at the start of this novel
Work: the Stars of Destiny are divided into 36 Heavenly Spirits and 72 Earthly Fiends in this novel
Work: a character drinks 18 bowls of wine and beats a tiger to death with his bare fists in this novel
Work: the erotic spinoff The Plum in the Golden Vase features minor characters from this novel
Work: Wu Song kills his brother's wife Pan Jinlian and her lover Ximen Qing in this novel
Work: Wu Song's left arm is lopped off by a magic sword, and he is nicknamed 'Pilgrim' after donning a Buddhist monk disguise in this novel
Work: Song Jiang leads 108 bandits from Mount Liang (Liangshan) marsh in this novel
Work: the protagonist kills his concubine Yan Poxi when she attempts to blackmail him in this novel
Work: Chao Gai is the first leader of the outlaws but dies before the Grand Assembly in this novel
Work: Gao Qiu rises from a street urchin to Grand Marshal after impressing a prince with his football skills in this novel
Work: Gao Qiu frames Lin Chong for attempted assassination in this novel
Work: Sidney Shapiro produced the most acclaimed translation of this novel, amalgamating its 70- and 100-chapter editions
Work: Pearl Buck translated this novel under the controversial title All Men Are Brothers
Work: this novel exists in 'simple' and 'complex' editions that diverge where the main characters are granted amnesty
Work: 68 of the 108 Stars of Destiny die in the campaign against the rebel Fang La in this novel
Work: a couple nicknamed 'the Ogress' and 'the gardener' run an inn where guests are killed and used as meat filling in this novel
Work: Utagawa Kuniyoshi's woodblock illustrations of this novel inspired a full-body tattoo craze
Work: a character caught eating beef without permission is cursed to walk at superhuman speed in this novel
Work: a character leaves his blind mother to fetch water and returns to find she has been eaten in this novel
Work: only three of the outlaws are women, one of whom owns the Black Inn, in this novel
Work: one of the Four Great Classical Novels of Chinese literature, attributed to Shi Nai'an
Work: Yi Sik claimed the Korean novel about Hong Gildong was written to imitate this Chinese novel
Work: this novel's opening is parodied in The Scholars when Wang Mien watches stars of the 'Scholars' constellation fall to earth
Work: a character is offered 100 gold pieces, accepts only one, then murders his mistress when she demands all 100 in this novel
